title: God's Love and Authority
intro: God's love and God's authority are not in tension with each other. They flow from the same source — His nature. He does not love because He must and rule because He can. He loves because He is love, and He reigns because all things belong to Him.
reading:
John 3:16 is perhaps the most quoted verse in all of Scripture — and still its weight has never been fully measured. God so loved the world that He gave His only Son. This was not obligation. It was overflowing, initiating, self-giving love. Romans 5:8 presses further: God demonstrates His love in that while we were still sinners, Christ died for us. Not after we improved. While we were still far.

1 John 4:8-10 declares that God is love — not merely that He is loving, but that love is the very substance of His being. And the measure of that love is not what He felt but what He did. He sent His Son. Love moved. Love acted. Love gave.

Psalm 103:11 places the scale of His love beyond measure: as high as the heavens are above the earth, so great is His steadfast love toward those who fear Him. Lamentations 3:22-23 reminds us that this love never ceases — His mercies never come to an end.

But this same God reigns with complete authority. Psalm 115:3 declares He does all that He pleases. Isaiah 46:10 announces that His counsel shall stand and He will accomplish all His purpose.

Here is the wonder of Gavin's outline: the One with all authority chose to use it to love you. Sovereignty and tenderness are not contradictions in God. They are two faces of the same perfect nature.
reflection: Do you tend to emphasize God's love at the expense of His authority, or His authority at the expense of His love? How does holding both together change the way you relate to Him?
abide: You are love and You are Lord. I do not have to choose between Your tenderness and Your sovereignty — they are one in You. Let me rest today in the arms of the God who rules all things and chose to love me.
